Cottage doorstep where garden meets forest, wildflowers growing through cracked path stones, neither tended nor wild.
Abandoned farmhouse kitchen, dust coating surfaces, single wildflower pushing through rotted floorboards toward light.
Garden gate left ajar, cultivated roses on one side, wild brambles pressing through from the other.
Village edge at dusk, last house before the forest, one candle flickering in the window.
Ruined stone wall where a garden once thrived, now half-reclaimed by encroaching forest growth.
Wooden bridge between village and woods, the halfway point where human territory surrenders to fae.
Attic of old cottage, dormer window framing the forest, abandoned toys gathering dust below.
Forest path transitioning from flagstones to packed dirt to moss, the border visible with each step.
Wishing well at crossroads, the exact point where four paths meet at the forest's edge.
Overgrown churchyard at village boundary, headstones leaning, ivy claiming stone and iron alike.
Market square at dawn before waking, empty stalls silent, morning mist drifting from the forest.
Abandoned nursery in cottage, empty crib beside window overlooking the dark forest beyond.
Millhouse threshold, water wheel half-broken, stream flowing from tamed pond into wild forest creek.
Orchard's last row of trees, cultivated apples giving way to wild crabapples and tangled undergrowth.
Shepherd's hut on moorland edge, domesticated pasture ending where the wild heath begins its reign.
Railway platform at forest border, tracks running from civilization into the green unknown.
Lighthouse keeper's cottage on cliff edge, between ordered village below and the untamed sea.
Cemetery gate standing open, consecrated ground inside, unmarked forest graves just beyond.
Town well in decaying square, cobblestones ending abruptly where wilderness reclaims the forgotten settlement.
Barn doorway facing fields, back entrance opening to forest, swallows nesting in the rafters between.
Schoolhouse steps overgrown, lesson books moldering inside, deer trails crossing the playground.
Manor house conservatory, glass walls dividing manicured gardens from the pressing wild forest.
Postal box at lane's end, letters for the village, the path beyond leading only to forest.
Tollbooth standing empty, road passing from paved commerce into dirt tracks through ancient woods.
Blacksmith's forge gone cold, village anvil silent, moss creeping across threshold toward forgotten tools.
